CENTRAL AUTOPSY SERVICE

After the Death of a Loved One

Guidance, answers, and gentle support — gathered in one place, for whenever you’re ready.
Watch the guide Download the booklet (PDF)
In a crisis or having thoughts of self-harm? Call or text 988, any hour.
START HERE

A 5-minute guide for families

Narrated by our team — what happens next, the autopsy explained, and how we support you.

▶ After the Death of a Loved One · 4:53
You don’t have to do this alone

A calm walk through the days ahead — gentle enough to watch in one sitting, and here whenever you want to return to it.

  • What happens in the first hours
  • What an autopsy is, and when the medical examiner is involved
  • Practical next steps, one at a time
  • Where to find grief and spiritual support

Contact the Autopsy Service

Reach our team with any question about your loved one’s care or an autopsy. We welcome your call.

You don’t have to carry this alone

Grief support at Henry Ford Health is open to everyone in our community — whether or not your loved one was in our care. Reach out whenever you’re ready.

QUESTIONS

Frequently asked

In most cases, no. An autopsy usually does not significantly delay funeral plans, and it does not prevent a viewing. Your loved one is treated with care, dignity and respect at every step.
